channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Republicans butt heads on daylight saving time after Trump directive
item.description: A Republican brouhaha is emerging after President-elect Trump called to eliminate daylight saving time, setting up battle lines between prominent GOP lawmakers in their hopes to lock the clock. Trump waded into the longtime sore subject Friday, surprising — and perplexing — some lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Indiana law shrouds executions in secrecy, prompting new pushes for public oversight
item.description: From public hangings in the town square to lethal injections witnessed by journalists, executions historically have mostly been carried out with at least some public scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man convicted of quadruple homicide is put to death in Indiana's 1st execution in 15 years
item.description: MICHIGAN CITY, Ind. (AP) — An Indiana man convicted of killing four people including his brother and his sister’s fiancé decades ago was put to death early Wednesday, marking the state’s first execution in 15 years. Joseph Corcoran, 49, was pronounced dead at 12:44 a.m.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Animal welfare group says bacterial infection killed Sonia the elephant in Pakistan
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Pakistani veterinary surgeons found that a bacterial infection killed an elephant at a safari park in the city of Karachi earlier this month, a veterinarian with a global animal welfare organization said Wednesday. Dr.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Amazon has up to 20% off gift cards ahead of the holidays
item.description: You can save on a wide range of gift cards, making it easier and more affordable to give the gift of shopping and saving.
item.dc:creator: Christina Marfice, KTLA 5

item.title: Community members wrestle with grief in aftermath of Wisconsin school shooting
item.description: Community members in Wisconsin are continuing to wrestle with grief and calling for change in the aftermath of a school shooting that killed a teacher and a student and wounded six others.
item.dc:creator: Devi Shastri And Scott Bauer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US to pay nearly $116M to settle lawsuits over rampant sexual abuse at California women's prison
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. government will pay nearly $116 million to resolve lawsuits brought by more than 100 women who say they were abused or mistreated at a now-shuttered federal prison in California that was known as the “rape club” because of rampant staff-on-inmate sexual misconduct.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and MICHAEL BALSAMO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biathlon official apologizes to athletes who have suffered mistreatment
item.description: The secretary general of the International Biathlon Union apologized to any athlete who was mistreated during his tenure at U.S. Biathlon while speaking to a 90-minute meeting Tuesday that focused on concerns about athlete safety brought to light by multiple Associated Press reports.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 1 dead, 9 injured in Maryland shooting and fiery car crash: police
item.description: One person has died and nine others were injured following a shooting and related fiery car crash in Maryland on Tuesday, police said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Chen, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Why shooting down mystery drones carries big risks
item.description: The buzz around the mysterious flurry of drones above New Jersey and other nearby states has prompted some officials, including President-elect Trump, to call for the U.S. military to shoot down the mystery objects. But such measures come with major risks, experts say.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walmart testing body cameras for some in-store employees
item.description: Some in-store Walmart employees are wearing body cameras as part of a pilot program, according to a company spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KTLA 5

item.title: Slaying of doctor was a murder-for-hire case, LA district attorney says
item.description: Five people have been arrested in connection with Mirshojae's murder, including his ex-wife, 53-year-old Ahang Kelk.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Department of Defense doubles down, says drones are not US military assets
item.description: The Pentagon doubled down Tuesday, saying drones flying over New Jersey and New York were not U.S. military assets and are likely being used by hobbyists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Popular fishing YouTuber Ben Milliken arrested for fishing tournament fraud
item.description: According to TPWD, their due diligence process discovered that Milliken didn't have a valid fishing license.
item.dc:creator: Michael Garcia, NewsNation

item.title: Shooting in Madison is the latest carnage at a US school or college over the past 25 years
item.description: Hundreds of people in the U.S. have died in shootings over the past 25 years in places like offices, stores and theaters, but the carnage perhaps hits hardest when it happens in schools and colleges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tom Cruise awarded highest Navy honor for a civilian
item.description: The 62-year-old actor was celebrated for his outstanding contributions to the Navy and the Marine Corps.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: After investigating Jan. 6, House GOP sides with Trump and goes after Liz Cheney
item.description: Wrapping up their report on the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol attack, House Republicans have concluded it’s former GOP Rep. Liz Cheney who should be prosecuted for her work probing the riot.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Suspect charged with killing UnitedHealthcare's CEO as an act of terrorism
item.description: The man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare's CEO has been charged with murder as an act of terrorism, prosecutors said Tuesday as they worked to bring him to a New York court from a Pennsylvania jail. Luigi Mangione already was charged with murder in the Dec.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: College hockey player sued by opponent over injury she suffered on the ice
item.description: A Michigan State University hockey player has been sued by a former opponent for injuries she suffered on the ice.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, ABC 4

item.title: Shock poll: 41 percent of young voters find killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO acceptable
item.description: Anger over health insurance companies has been in the spotlight after Thompson was fatally shot Dec. 4 in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: 48 memorial signs to honor fallen Ohio firefighters, officers
item.description: "I think this effort shows an appreciation for the men and women who serve selflessly in the service of others without thinking about harm to them or their families," said Dayton Police Chief Kamran Afzal.
item.dc:creator: Aaliyah Seabrooks, NewsNation

item.title: Oscar Mayer accepting applications for the newest class of Wienermobile drivers
item.description: Oscar Mayer is now accepting applications for its coveted “Hotdogger” position, offering recent college graduates the chance to drive the brand’s iconic 27-foot Wienermobile across the country.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: NBA unveils new All-Star format featuring four-team tournament
item.description: The NBA 's All-Star Game is going to be an All-Star tournament this season, with the league announcing on Tuesday that it has finalized plans to use a different format for the upcoming midseason showcase in San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Montana judge blocks rule that prevented transgender people from changing their sex on documents
item.description: HELENA, Mont. (AP) — A state judge in Montana has temporarily blocked policies that prevented transgender people from changing the sex designation on their birth certificates and driver’s licenses.
item.dc:creator: AMY BETH HANSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Grammy award-winning gospel singer arrested in Greenville
item.description: GREENVILLE, S.C. (WSPA) - Grammy award-winning gospel singer Le'Andria Johnson was arrested Monday afternoon in Greenville for public intoxication. The City of Greenville confirmed with 7NEWS that Johnson was charged with public intoxication and an open container on a public street.
item.dc:creator: Nikolette Miller, Fox 5

item.title: California couple on vacation fatally shot in Mexico, officials say
item.description: An American couple from Northern California was shot and killed by gunmen in the western Mexican state of Michoacán, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KRON4

item.title: Jalen Hurts accounts for 3 TDs and Eagles beat Steelers 27-13 for franchise-best 10th straight win
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Jalen Hurts absorbed the shots when the Eagles' recent anemic passing attack was panned by his own star wide receivers. Hurts and A.J.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: California DMV apologizes for Cybertruck's license plate but family cites misinterpretation
item.description: On Thursday, the license plate in question, which was on a Tesla Cybertruck, was spotlighted in a post to X by StopAntisemitism, a group “dedicated to fighting anti-Semitism.” The family, however, argues it is being misread.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, ABC 4

item.title: Romney: ‘You can’t complain’ if Trump does what he says he would do
item.description: “Donald Trump won. He won overwhelmingly. He said what he was going to do, and that’s what he’s doing,” Romney said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: Spotify too repetitive? Here's how to retrain the algorithm
item.description: Over the past couple years, complaints have piled up in online forums from music lovers having trouble discovering new music. Why? No matter what they do, Spotify seems to be serving them the same old songs, again and again.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Private Sin: How a church protected child molesters
item.description: Protestant Reformed Churches in America is struggling with what it has called the "private sin" of child sexual abuse and decades of covering up for abusers, including church leaders and school teachers.
item.dc:creator: Ken Kolker, Fox 5

item.title: Freddie Freeman's walk-off grand slam ball sells for $1.56 million at auction
item.description: Freddie Freeman’s historic walk-off grand slam ball from Game 1 of the World Series has sold for $1.56 million at auction.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Holiday shopping scams to avoid this winter
item.description: Waiting on a plethora of present-related notifications? Don't assume all emails and texts you get are legitimate.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: Photos: US storms bring heavy snow, dangerous ice and a tornado in California
item.description: In San Francisco, authorities issued the first-ever tornado warning. Meanwhile, upstate New York saw heavy snow, the Midwest encountered an ice storm, and severe weather warnings were issued around Lake Tahoe.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 6 ways to avoid a financial holiday hangover
item.description: The festivity of December is replaced all too quickly by the due dates of January, when the bills from holiday spending and travel arrive.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Palmer, ABC 4

item.title: A look back at the most notable bankruptcies of 2024
item.description: This year saw its fair share of bankruptcies, with household names and century-old companies in the mix.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, ABC 4

item.title: 2 cats die from suspected bird flu traced to raw milk in California
item.description: Health officials are investigating possible cases of H5 bird flu after two cats died from consuming recalled raw milk, according to the Los Angeles County Department of Public Health.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, WGN 9
